column1: # Public EC/XRAM names are recovered from the official GCU Service and
# A small number of internal fields are named only where firmware data flow
# establishes their role directly. Ranges name the first byte in Ghidra; the
# export script also emits the complete range in its register index.

column4: Shared two-byte SMBus word-data staging buffer used by charger and battery transactions
Battery telemetry-cache voltage request consumed as big-endian high/low bytes and derated before publication to EC[0x0522:0x0523]; underlying telemetry transaction is not yet recovered
One-byte capacity scaling/calibration factor seeded by vendor battery command 0x42 and subsequently clamped or adjusted by the EC capacity state machine
Battery design capacity, uint16 little-endian, mAh
Last full capacity, uint16 little-endian, mAh
Battery design voltage, uint16 little-endian, mV
Battery discharge current, uint16 little-endian, mA
Battery remaining capacity, uint16 little-endian, mAh
Battery voltage, uint16 little-endian, mV
EC firmware version
Platform system identifier
Silent-mode status
Main fan tachometer, uint16
BIOS information byte 5
Second fan tachometer, uint16
BIOS function flags
EC sub-version
Power-source state
Battery status; bits 7:6 encode a 2-, 3- or 4-cell series pack for voltage calculations
Battery alert flags
Shared battery data-session flags; bit 0 is set after telemetry/cache initialization and cleared when the battery state machine restarts, while other bits have independent state-machine meanings
BIOS information byte 3
Battery temperature, uint16 little-endian, 0.1 K
Battery cycle count, uint16 little-endian
Relative state of charge, percent
Signed little-endian battery current in mA; positive while charging and negative while discharging, with the magnitude published to EC[0x0434:0x0435]
EC-selected battery charge-voltage limit, uint16 little-endian, mV
Read by a separate helper at CODE:D1AE and bank-wrapper callers; raw CODE:D1D1 does not call this helper, so it is not the direct aging-voltage gate previously inferred
Bit-0 power-source debounce counter; seeded with 200 or 1 by CODE:14E8 and decremented by CODE:1444 before EC[0x0490].0 changes
Bit-1 session-start debounce counter; ROM initialization table seeds it to 5, CODE:3C69 reloads it to 3 and CODE:3B32 decrements it before setting EC[0x0490].1
Bit-1/bit-2 session-stop debounce counter; ROM initialization table seeds it to 5 and CODE:3C69 decrements it before clearing EC[0x0490].1/.2
Power-source bit-5 debounce counter; ROM initialization table seeds it to 5 and the CODE:3BB3 path decrements it before setting EC[0x0490].5
Power-source bit-5/bit-6 clear debounce counter; ROM initialization table seeds it to 5 and the CODE:3CE0 path decrements it before clearing EC[0x0490].5/.6
0-7 protection level raised above the battery discharge-current threshold and lowered below the 70 percent hysteresis threshold
ACPI BTP0 battery remaining-capacity trip point, uint16 little-endian
AP/BIOS control byte
Custom-mode bit 7 and AC-recovery bit 3
Custom light bit 6 and PL4-double bit 7
Platform project identifier
AP-present bit 0; also fan-alert byte
Hardware support flags 5
MyFan2 PWM levels or configurable-TGP fields
Light-bar mode flags
Light-bar red, green and blue values
OEM service project identifier
Fn-lock bit 4
Office, Gaming, Turbo, Custom and FanBoost mode selector
CPU VRM sustained-current limit, A
CPU VRM peak-current limit, A
Main fan duty, raw value approximately percent times two
Second fan duty, raw value approximately percent times two
Secondary command/trigger flags
FanBoost, light bar, Win-lock and other capabilities
Silent mode, USB charging, RGB keyboard and battery protection
Win-lock, USB-charge and related action trigger
Win-lock, FanBoost and related action status
RGB keyboard current and default RGB components
Official optional RGB-keyboard music-mode selector; 0xFE enables and 0x00 disables it on supported variants, while current project EC[0x0766].2 reports unsupported
EC ROM identifier
QKey/default-mode/Fan3 support; meaning is platform dependent
Current PL1, PL2 and PL4 values
TCC temperature target/enable or legacy MyFan3 PWM
Fan transition interval or legacy MyFan3 PWM
Legacy MyFan3 default PWM values
GPU D-state or MyFan3 GPU setting
Single-zone keyboard backlight control
Backlight and battery-protection capability flags
Minimum speed, minimum temperature and extra speed
BIOS OEM byte 3
AP/BIOS flags including QC Fn-lock
AP OEM byte 3
Battery protection mode bits 5:4; also touchpad LED on other platforms
Battery-saver PL1, PL2, PL4 and D defaults
CCI mode index
CCI mode profiles 1 through 3
Charge upper threshold, percent
Independent fan-control flags
AP fan-management enable
AP OEM bytes 7 and 8
BIOS OEM byte 8
Composite power state
Charge lower threshold, percent
GPU and main module identifiers
Gaming, Office and Turbo TCC offsets
External liquid-cooling fan and pump
WKD port-ID change control
Charger commands requested for a later retry; bits 0 and 1 select ChargingCurrent and ChargingVoltage
Charger command work flags consumed by the SMBus queue worker
ChargingVoltage word copied byte-for-byte from EC[0x0522:0x0523] before the charger SMBus command is queued, uint16 little-endian
0-25 step counter in the shared optional-RGB template; used with EC[0x076F] to index waveform tables and update XRAM[0x1803/0x1805/0x1808], but current project reports no RGB-keyboard capability
Bit 0 is a debounced mirror of GPIO data-mirror GPD3; IT5571 D-board pin use is unverified
Five-tick debounce counter for XRAM[0x0858] bit 0
Prescaler counter for the battery high-voltage stress accumulator
Second-stage counter for qualified high-voltage windows

Temperature-weighted battery high-voltage stress accumulator, uint16 big-endian; reset startup does not initialize this XRAM word, so its value is indeterminate until CODE:D1D1 observes EC[0x0490].1 clear
Shared scratch byte reused across firmware domains; the battery-aging path locally stores caller seed 1 here and compares it with auxiliary derating thresholds before other code can replace it
Temporary SMBus controller/channel index used by the common transaction routine
Temporary SMBus transaction protocol/mode byte; charger word writes use 0x0C
Temporary 8-bit SMBus slave address; charger transactions use 0x12
Four-byte SMBus command/data descriptor; word transactions store command, zero, data pointer high, data pointer low
Common SMBus result sentinel: 0x00 on completion without controller error, 0xEE on timeout or controller error
Raw SMBus host-controller status captured while polling for transaction completion
Boolean result returned in R7 by the common SMBus transaction routine: 1 success, 0 failure
Single-zone lighting mode
CPU fan curve rising-temperature thresholds
CPU fan curve falling-temperature thresholds
CPU fan duty table, raw value is percent times two
Second/GPU fan rising-temperature thresholds
Second/GPU fan falling-temperature thresholds
Second/GPU fan duty table, raw value is percent times two
RamFan1p5 table status 1
RamFan1p5 table status 2
RamFan1p5 table apply/control byte
SMFI HRAMWC; selects H2RAM path and enables four host-visible lower-4K DLM windows
SMFI HRAMW0BA; H2RAM window 0 base bits 11:4
SMFI HRAMW1BA; H2RAM window 1 base bits 11:4
SMFI HRAMW0AAS; window 0 size and half-window read/write protection
SMFI HRAMW1AAS; window 1 size and half-window read/write protection
SMFI HRAMW2BA; H2RAM window 2 base bits 11:4
SMFI HRAMW3BA; H2RAM window 3 base bits 11:4
SMFI HRAMW2AAS; window 2 size and half-window read/write protection
SMFI HRAMW3AAS; window 3 size and half-window read/write protection
Host-to-EC command byte; handlers observed for 0x22, 0x23 and 0x24
SPCTRL1 including I2ECCTRL bits 1:0: 0 disabled, 2 read-only, 3 read-write
SPCTRL2 bit 0 enables dedicated I2EC port address decoding
Dedicated I2EC LPC I/O port base high byte
Dedicated I2EC LPC I/O port base low byte, aligned to four ports
